title: How Marco Rubio Is Running Venezuela From Afar
summary: The secretary of state effectively controls Venezuela’s finances, the distribution of its natural resources and its government. His grip on the country is a vivid manifestation of American power in the Trump era.

title: Times Journalists Subpoenaed as Trump Escalates Pressure on Media
summary: The Justice Department is seeking to compel testimony from reporters who wrote about the new Air Force One. The Times called the move a “brazen act.”

title: Bipartisan Housing Bill Becomes Law Even Though Trump Refuses to Sign It
summary: After a 10-day clock, the housing bill turned into law at midnight without the president’s signature. But his decision not to sign reflects a growing rift between him and Senate Republicans.

title: Platner’s Rise and Fall Revives Old Questions About ‘Bernie Bros’ and Women
summary: The collapse of Graham Platner’s Senate bid in Maine after a rape allegation renewed attention to a movement built by Senator Bernie Sanders that some say is too forgiving of male misconduct.

title: How The Times Reported on Graham Platner
summary: Two senior editors discuss the difficult effort to track down allegations against the leading Democrat for Senate in Maine and the questions that soon followed.

title: An American Politician is Blocked by Israeli Settlers in the West Bank
summary: Representative Ro Khanna was barred from leaving for 90 minutes. Where past U.S. leaders toured the region to show support for Israel, today’s Democratic presidential aspirants are going to bolster their credentials as critics.

title: Israel Struck an Iranian Steel Facility. Was It a Valid Military Target?
summary: During the war, Israel attacked Iran’s steel plants, saying they provided forces with revenue and the means to make weapons, but it also hurt the civilian economy.

title: Iran’s Top Diplomat Is in Oman for Talks on Strait of Hormuz
summary: The visit comes after heavy clashes this week between the U.S. and Iran. On Saturday, the country’s supreme leader vowed revenge for his father’s killing.

title: ICE Killing in Houston Puts Focus on Surge in Immigration Arrests
summary: The shooting death of Lorenzo Salgado Araujo occurred as immigration enforcement has ramped up across the country, with thousands being arrested daily.

title: Houston to Investigate How ICE Agent Came to Kill a Mexican Immigrant
summary: The agency said Lorenzo Salgado Araujo tried to ram agents with a van before one shot him dead. A lawyer for his passengers said that was untrue.

title: Buckling Manhattan High Rise Becomes Focus of Criminal Investigation
summary: The inquiry, being conducted by the New York City Department of Investigation and the Manhattan district attorney’s office, was in an early stage, and its focus was unclear.

title: Developer of Troubled Midtown High-Rise Had Tried to Avoid the Spotlight
summary: Nathan Berman has shied away from the showy ways necessary to succeed in New York real estate. Now, his largest project is in limbo.

title: Mick Jagger Knows He May Have Played His Last Rolling Stones Show
summary: The legendary rock star, now 82, on how fame, touring and aging have changed him.

title: We’re Living in a Tick Nightmare. It’s Time to Go to War.
summary: With ticks, we’ve barely begun to fight back. The longer we wait, the more territory they will claim across both our geographic and psychic maps.

title: ‘Platner Is Vile.’ McConnell Is Missing. Welcome to American Politics.
summary: What America’s embarrassments have in common.

title: Gravitational Pull
summary: We think and dream and fuss and project entire worlds into existence. Meanwhile, the earth’s orbit continues and the sun goes on shining.

title: Historian Kerri K. Greenidge and Her Book ‘The Grimkes’ Come Under Fire
summary: Kerri K. Greenidge appeared to lose her professorship at Tufts University after scholars began scrutinizing her 2022 book, “The Grimkes,” which is no longer listed on its publisher’s website.
